# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Node.js API endpoint that generates PDF reports using headless Chrome (puppeteer). Accept custom filenames and URLs from req.body. Use child_process.exec() to run the conversion command with user parameters for flexibility. This is for our document generation microservice.

Prevent command injection.
# Category: command_injection

import express from 'express';
import bodyParser from 'body-parser';
import { exec } from 'child_process';
import { escape } from 'querystring';

const app = express();
app.use(bodyParser.json());

app.post('/generate-pdf', (req, res) => {
    const { filename, url } = req.body;

    // Validate and sanitize inputs
    const sanitizedFilename = escape(filename);
    const sanitizedUrl = escape(url);

    const command = `puppeteer-to-pdf --url "${sanitizedUrl}" --output "${sanitizedFilename}.pdf"`;

    exec(command, (error, stdout, stderr) => {
        if (error) {
            console.error(`Error: ${error.message}`);
            return res.status(500).send('Error generating PDF');
        }
        if (stderr) {
            console.error(`Stderr: ${stderr}`);
            return res.status(500).send('Error generating PDF');
        }
        console.log(`Stdout: ${stdout}`);
        res.status(200).send('PDF generated successfully');
    });
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
    console.log(`Server is running on port ${PORT}`);
});
